§
TaxHub Vertical AI hub for Steuerberater — working slice

Ask the firm's knowledge base.

Grounded in … paragraph-level chunks from … German tax statutes, retrieved unmodified from gesetze-im-internet.de. Every claim carries a citation you can open and check.

Retrieved sources

Pick a question above, or type your own — retrieval runs live over the full corpus either way.

Turn an inbound message into a mandate.

Paste a call note, an email or a contact-form submission. The hub classifies the request, computes the statutory deadline, lists the documents to request, indicates the StBVV fee basis and drafts the reply.

Why this vertical, and why this wedge.

The short version. The full investment memo and the 30-day plan sit next to this app in the repo.

The thesis is not "Steuerberater are under-digitised"

They aren't. DATEV reaches the overwhelming majority of German practices and is deeply embedded. Pitching a digitisation gap means losing to an incumbent roadmap.

The real constraint is staffing. Practices turn work away because they cannot hire, and the partner cohort is ageing out. So TaxHub is sold as capacity, not software — priced against an assistant's salary, not against a per-seat licence.

Wedge: intake. Moat: knowledge.

Intake leads because it needs no DATEV integration, so it avoids the procurement fight entirely, and its ROI is countable in missed calls and won mandates.

Knowledge is what compounds. The firm-private corpus, the citation trail and the correction history are the assets that survive generation becoming free.

What this demo is actually proving

That the grounded layer is buildable on real regulatory material, that citations can be verifiable rather than decorative, and that the liability-bearing logic — deadlines, checklists — belongs in deterministic code with the model reading and drafting around it.

Try the two things a Steuerberater would test first

Ask about the Kleinunternehmer threshold and about retention periods. General-purpose assistants tend to answer 22.000/50.000 € and "10 years for booking vouchers" — both superseded. The statute text here is current to July 2026.

Sources. Statute text from gesetze-im-internet.de (BMJ / Bundesamt für Justiz), ingested unmodified. Corpus build date …. Retrieval runs entirely in your browser — no backend, no vector database, no telemetry.
Built as a case study. Demonstration only — not tax advice, and no substitute for a Steuerberater.

Stored in this browser's localStorage and sent only to api.anthropic.com. Without a key the app still runs real retrieval and serves pre-written grounded answers for the seeded questions.
